question.text: Under Regulation 33(3) of SEBI LODR 2015, within what period must quarterly financial results be submitted to stock exchanges?

answer.text: Within 45 days for all quarters including the last quarter
Within 45 days for the first three quarters and 60 days for the last quarter

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 33(3) requires financial results to be submitted within 45 days from the end of each of the first three quarters, and 60 days for the last quarter. The additional 15 days for Q4 accounts for the annual audit requirement. **The Trap:** Candidates often remember only one deadline (either 45 or 60 days) without noting the distinction between interim and final quarter results. The last quarter gets extra time because annual audited results require more processing. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX covers financial disclosure requirements. Series II-A tests this as RTAs need to be aware of when price-sensitive financial information will be published. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Financial results are price-sensitive information. The period just before the 45-day or 60-day deadline is when companies typically announce results. RTAs should be especially vigilant about insider trading compliance during these windows.
